books.title: Open Infrastructure and the Sociology of Knowledge: Rethinking Scholarly Communication
books.abstract: This book examines how scholarly infrastructure shapes epistemic communities and explores pathways toward community-governed, open alternatives to commercial publishing platforms.

articles.title: Epistemic Justice and Open Access: A Philosophical Analysis
articles.venue: Journal of the Philosophy of Science
articles.abstract: This article analyzes open-access publishing through the lens of epistemic justice, arguing that paywalled scholarship systematically disadvantages researchers in the Global South.

articles.title: AI Agents in Academic Workflows: Opportunities and Epistemic Risks
articles.abstract: An examination of how autonomous AI agents are beginning to mediate knowledge production workflows and the implications for reproducibility and epistemic accountability.

conf.title: Towards a FAIR Metadata Framework for Open Infrastructure
conf.abstract: We propose a metadata validation framework grounded in FAIR principles, designed for federated open-access repositories.
